flags.defaults_note: all remaining llama-server options at 8f114a9 build defaults (fresh process for this phase so the 'cancel task' log census is clean)

model.tokenizer: gguf-embedded (Qwen2 BPE as packaged in the checkpoint; identity covered by the model-file sha256 above)

hardware.instance_type: local-dev-host (linux/amd64, 4 CPU cores, 15 GiB RAM; llama-server -t 4, infergate, and inferbench co-located on the same cores)

gateway.version: 74f2372 (infergate@74f2372acea62645fa3c1d91689574ea9de7c589, host binary built read-only via git archive; binary sha256 in evidence/i3/logs/build-info.txt)
gateway.config_version: v1-24d2072b (scenarios/b/config/gateway-llamacpp.json: llamacpp backend, no fallback, upstream_timeout 300s, stream_write_timeout 30s)

client.location: same host (inferbench on host -> infergate 127.0.0.1:18080 -> llama-server 127.0.0.1:18082, loopback; client shares the 4 CPU cores with the engine)

hypothesis: Cancellation vs the real engine: client-side cancellation at 8 observed output tokens propagates through the gateway to llama-server for every realized cancel -- llama-server logs 'srv stop: cancel task' once per canceled request and the serving slot is observed free (25 ms /slots polling) within 2.5 s of the client-side cancel completion. The 2.5 s bound is the ENGINE's release granularity, not gateway latency: llama-server notices an abort at the next decode-batch boundary, which can be one concurrent prefill ubatch away (infergate IG-T005 direct measurement @74f2372: mid-stream release 1.25-5.24 ms; pre-first-token up to 2.19 s at prefill-batch boundaries).
notes: I3 cancellation-verification run, not a benchmark (100% deliberate cancels). Requests the model ends by EOS before the 8th token complete ok and are accounted as completion/cancel non-events, mirroring the Scenario A race accounting.
